title: agropyron caesium subsp. koeleri
intro: Agropyron caesium subsp. koeleri, also known as Koeler's blue grass, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its blue-green foliage and delicate spikes of purple flowers. Traditionally, Koeler's blue grass has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Koeler's blue grass is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and can also be used in tincture or powder form for its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
